item.title: Judge halts Hegseth’s bid to punish Sen. Kelly for video message to troops
item.description: The Trump administration intends to appeal the ruling, which says the retired Navy officer’s right to free speech was under attack.

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez, Rubio to offer dueling visions of world order in Munich
item.description: The potential 2028 presidential candidates will sound off on a range of burgeoning crises as European leaders recoil at America’s populist politics.

item.title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
item.description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington ...

item.title: White House fires U.S. attorney in N.Y. hours after judges appointed him
item.description: Donald T. Kinsella was chosen by judges to lead the federal prosecutors’ office in Albany, then fired hours later via an email from the White House.

item.title: Justice Dept’s head of antitrust departs amid tensions on enforcement
item.description: Gail Slater — an antitrust attorney who vowed to resist political interference at her confirmation hearing — announced her departure on social media.
